what_it_does: A dated (2026-06-10) overnight-build mandate document. It restates Tim's verbal goal as an actionable bar, defines the concrete 'morning experience' the autonomous loop aims to deliver (5 numbered outcomes), enumerates a work queue (S1-S6) with file-level build targets, and lists binding 'loop laws' that govern each autonomous loop fire.
observations: Opens by quoting Tim's literal words ('blow my fricking mind') then offers a 'best version of that intent' as the operating goal.
States a 5-point 'morning experience' bar: he is greeted, the builder is in the surface, a memory returns by itself, the night presents itself, the night's ideas enter the lifecycle.
Each morning-bar item carries an honest scoping caveat (e.g. 'condition partially fires — the panel exists, the full UI doesn't').

Work queue S1-S6 names specific files (runtime/ui_claude_session.py), CLI invocations (claude -p --output-format stream-json), API routes (/api/claude/start|continue|history), and env gates (COMPANY_PANEL_PERMISSION default plan).

Loop laws bind every fire: one bounded beat, lock-or-exit (loop.lock <25min), commit-per-unit to main with '[overnight]' prefix and NO Co-Authored-By, verify-by-USE, FLOOR UNCHANGED, fail-loud, no green-paint, GPU-aware.
Distinguishes an agent-facing record (.build/overnight/MORNING.md) from 'TIM's copy' which is the surface itself.
Repeatedly asserts the operator/MCP face boundary: /api/claude/* never on the MCP face, panel launches claude only operator-initiated.
standouts: The 'no green-paint, ever' / 'feel/aesthetics → needs-tim' honesty discipline woven through both the bar and the laws.
The 'FLOOR UNCHANGED' invariant: the loop itself never self-dispatches builds; autonomous claude stays lead-only; nothing auto-resolves.
Explicit 'state lives in files, never in any one context window' — compaction-survival as a design premise.
conventions: Uses the repo's altitude vocabulary (Pulse/River), surface/floor/gate vocabulary, and address forms (ui://).
Follows a mandate/criteria style: a goal restatement, a concrete bar, a queue, and binding laws.
Commit-law convention is explicitly overridden for this loop (NO Co-Authored-By, '[overnight]' prefix) versus the repo default.
notes: If briefing someone: this is the charter for one overnight autonomous build loop — the goal, the felt-morning bar, the S1-S6 file-level queue, and the laws every loop fire must obey.
questions: Were S1-S6 actually built, and which morning-bar items reached 'felt' vs stayed partial? (the file states intent, not outcome)
What is the relationship of this single-night mandate to the longer-lived build skills referenced elsewhere in the repo?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — it is titled a mandate and reads as a complete mandate: goal, target experience, queue, and governing laws.
apparent_themes: Felt outcome over reported tasks ('a changed morning, not a list')
Honesty / anti-green-paint and explicit scoping caveats
Operator-vs-MCP face separation and an immovable safety floor
Resolution at the operator's altitude (greeting, not a log)
intention_signals: An attempt to give an autonomous overnight agent loop a north star that is both ambitious (mind-blowing morning) and disciplined (reversible, honest, floor-preserving), so it can run unsupervised across compaction boundaries and still arrive at a coherent, trustworthy result.
novelty: The 'morning experience' framing — defining success as a felt arrival state for one specific person rather than a feature checklist — plus the hard 'no green-paint' honesty law attached to a fully autonomous loop.
contribution: Contributes a concrete operating charter for an overnight autonomous build: the felt-morning success bar, the S1-S6 file-level queue, and a complete set of loop laws (lock, commit-form, verify-by-USE, floor-unchanged, no-green-paint, GPU-aware) that a fused system could keep as the template for unattended build runs.
summary_for_embedding: A dated overnight autonomous-build mandate for the Company that defines a five-point felt morning-experience bar, an S1-S6 file-level work queue, and binding per-fire loop laws including verify-by-use, floor-unchanged, and no-green-paint.
intention_for_embedding: To direct an unsupervised overnight agent loop toward delivering a coherent, honest, reversible 'changed morning' for Tim rather than a list of completed tasks.
